programmes.show_description: Sins of the Fathers: Another perplexing mystery for the chalk and cheese detectives. A village priest is found dead shortly after performing a highly-publicised miracle cure.
Trust: Tracey falls for her smooth therapist Gerald and argues with Darryl about the lack of trust in their relationship when he bans her from continuing her sessions.
I Defy Thee to Forget: The Sixth Precinct's search for the murderer of an officer is derailed as Annie creates a fissure in the Corcoran home.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Thirty Something: Sibling sitcom and the Essex girls have the birthday blues. Sharon is approaching her 30th birthday but is unable to see a bright future ahead of her.
Crash Landing: A plane makes a crash-landing in Sun Hill, plunging Daly into an investigation in which his ex-lover plays a crucial role.
New. Attack of the Centorts - Part One: A psychiatric patient runs away from hospital, convinced an alien race is on its way. Archie threatens to tell Julia about Daniel's escort activities.
New. Attack of the Centorts - Part Two: Time is running out for Mia as the past catches up with her. Nick makes a breakthrough and it's decision day for Vivien.
The Kindness of Strangers: George collapses again, so Ed pulls strings to make him a priority. Diane flirts with a locum doctor and Rosie receives legal papers outlining Sami's plans.
Nice and Easy Does It: Charlie steals drugs from the hospital as he prepares to honour Megan's wishes and end her suffering. Tess discovers what he's done and they consider other options.
Crash Landing: A plane makes a crash-landing in Sun Hill, plunging Daly into an investigation in which his ex-lover plays a crucial role.
New. There is fierce competition in the Vic as Roxy holds auditions for new bar staff, while Amira's attempt to seduce Syed leads her into dangerous territory.
New. As the new Mitchell brother causes tension, Ronnie is horrified by Jack's deception. Amira struggles to cope with her guilt, and Bianca receives an unexpected lifeline.
Barks and Bites: Baaa-rilliant comedy and drama in the historic series following vets on call in Yorkshire. Tristan and James examine a consignment of sheep for export.
Leo's disappointed to finish second in a speedboat race, and takes out his anger on the winner. Avril's haunted by nightmares about the plane crash, and Ken loses his temper with Sarah.
Nothing Will Come of Nothing: The team investigate the disappearance of a local lothario from a casino run by two eccentric sisters.
Pardon Me: Classic prison-set sitcom with the great Ronnie Barker and a guest appearance from David Jason. Fletch decides to help an old lag who has been in prison for 17 years.
The Swan Man of Ilkley: Popular sitcom that first graced our screens in 1973. Lennie from the pickle factory has visions of sailing the canals in a swan boat. Can his friends help?
Watching the Clock: Much loved sitcom. Clegg climbs a tree to prove a point and finds that what goes up doesn't always come down. Meanwhile, the police deal with a runaway cooker!
Has Anyone Seen a Peruvian Wart?: Roy Clarke's enduringly popular sitcom. Tempted by her baking, a renowned romancer of gullible women focuses his attentions on the widow Nora Batty.
Death on the Close Part 1: When a man with no ID or connection to Marlow is found dead in the middle of a suburban road, Judith, Becks and Suzie investigate.
Death on the Close Part 2: A link to a past missing persons case shines a new light on the residents of Linnet Close - but could they be responsible for murder on their doorstep?
Likely Lad James Bolam and Minder's Dennis Waterman star in another adventure for the aging detectives. The squad try to trace the attacker of an Asian woman in Southall.
Nothing Will Come of Nothing: The team investigate the disappearance of a local lothario from a casino run by two eccentric sisters.
For Love nor Money: Rising star Colin Buchanan is one of the hard-nosed cops. A retired detective is found dead on his local golf course, and Pascoe uncovers a tale of bribery.
Thirty Something: Sibling sitcom and the Essex girls have the birthday blues. Sharon is approaching her 30th birthday but is unable to see a bright future ahead of her.
A Morning Song: Five Points falls into a state of panic as a community safe haven is seized by one of the deadliest men in New York.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Love on the Run: More Essex comedy from the Chigwell sisters. Darryl has been taken to hospital with appendicitis and plans to escape and spend the night with his wife.
Father's Day: Boyden's estranged daughter asks him for protection. Boyden helps her out and they start to rekindle their relationship.
New. Tuning Out: Vivien is back to work at the Mill, while Joe's daughter Emily makes a visit from America to stay with her father and Daniel.
New. School's Out: George and Ronnie attend marriage counselling, and Nick faces a new challenge. An attack on a schoolboy forces his teacher to make a tough decision.
The Heart of the Matter: A heart becomes available, so Ed tries to ensure his father is the recipient by lying about the results of his blood test. Diane wants to know where she stands.
What Tonight Means to Me Part 1: A power cut plunges Holby into darkness just as Lenny prepares to accept the Jafa fellowship. Jordan discovers Ludlow's reason for the drug trial.
Father's Day: Boyden's estranged daughter asks him for protection. Boyden helps her out and they start to rekindle their relationship.
New. As Dr Al bids farewell to the Square, a familiar face returns. Becca has a cunning plan to get Stacey's stall back, and Tiff finds herself in danger. Is the game up for Syed?
New. Jack is stunned by a new piece of evidence - has he found Archie's murderer? Meanwhile, Syed is pressured by Zainab, and Bianca welcomes her little brother back to the Square.
The Bull with the Bowler Hat: Fun with the animal experts working in Yorkshire. While Tristan teaches artificial insemination, Siegfried only succeeds in enraging a bull.
Jan meets an interesting man at a fashion show in Rome. Tom and Sir Edward come to blows, and Kate persuades Jack to take up gambling again.
In My Memory Lock'd: Frank and Lu struggle to unlock the memories of an amnesiac with a mysterious connection to an exclusive hotel where no one recognises him.
A Test of Character: The immortal prison-set sitcom. When Godber studies to get another O-level before leaving jail, Fletch cannot understand his appetite for all the hard work.
Hermione (The Short Course): Young-at-heart sitcom. Howard is alarmed that his love life may feature in Clegg's memoirs. Smiler is aghast when a potential relationship suddenly ends.
Who's That Mouse in the Poetry Group?: Roy Clarke's record breaking sitcom. Smiler pretends to be an intellectual in an attempt to enchant Marina. Howard wants to keep an eye on them.
Available for Weddings: The world's longest-running sitcom! Cycling down a hillside, Clegg ends up with a fractured foot. Truly demonstrates how the accident could have been avoided.
New. Death by Vinyl: First Chapter: Members of Cat's old band reunite to record an album at a retreat not far from Wildermarsh - and it's not long before one of them turns up dead.
New. Full Moon Fever: A terrible night shift leaves the station under investigation. Grace doubts her career choice when Cal is arrested for resisting a racially discriminatory stop and search.
Another gripping case for our detectives using 1970s methods to combat crimes. The team re-investigate a bungled murder case where the body of a young girl was misidentified.
In My Memory Lock'd: Frank and Lu struggle to unlock the memories of an amnesiac with a mysterious connection to an exclusive hotel where no one recognises him.
